highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.126 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release adds a dynamic model picker that reads available models from your gateway's /v1/models endpoint when using a custom ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, making it easier to switch between models on compatible gateways. A new `claude project purge [path]` command lets you cleanly wipe all Claude Code state for a project — transcripts, tasks, file history, and config entries — which is handy when starting fresh or cleaning up sensitive sessions.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.122 Released
highlights.summary: This release introduces the `ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_SERVICE_TIER` environment variable, allowing AWS Bedrock users to select between `default`, `flex`, or `priority` service tiers — useful for controlling cost and latency on enterprise workloads. It also adds a handy quality-of-life improvement: pasting a Pull Request URL directly into the `/resume` search box now finds the session that originally created that PR.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.121 Released
highlights.summary: A new `alwaysLoad` option in MCP server config ensures that tools from a specified server are always available without going through tool-search deferral — great for MCP servers you rely on in every session. The release also adds `claude plugin prune` to remove orphaned plugin dependencies, keeping your environment tidy, plus a type-to-filter feature for faster navigation.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest and most capable model in the Opus family, which is directly accessible through Claude Code. Developers using Claude Code can switch to Opus 4.7 via the `/model` picker to leverage its improved reasoning and coding capabilities on complex tasks.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial offers a concise but comprehensive walkthrough of Claude Code's core features, designed to get you productive quickly without hours of watching. It's a strong choice if you've installed Claude Code but feel unsure about how to structure prompts, manage sessions, or integrate it into your daily VS Code workflow.

highlights.title: Uber torches 2026 AI budget on Claude Code in four months
highlights.summary: Reports indicate Uber burned through its entire 2026 AI tooling budget on Claude Code usage in just four months, highlighting how quickly costs can escalate with agentic AI coding assistants at scale. This is a cautionary tale for teams adopting Claude Code broadly — setting spending limits, usage policies, and monitoring token consumption early is critical before rolling out org-wide.

highlights.title: Who owns the code Claude Code wrote?
highlights.summary: This article dives into the murky legal territory of copyright ownership for AI-generated code, specifically when that code is produced by Claude Code in a professional context. Developers and teams should be aware of their organization's IP policies and Anthropic's terms of service before shipping AI-generated code in commercial products.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot is moving to usage-based billing and retiring annual plans
highlights.summary: GitHub is shifting Copilot Pro and Pro+ from annual subscriptions to usage-based billing, signaling a broader industry move toward pay-per-use AI coding tools — similar to how Claude Code is billed. Developers currently weighing Claude Code vs. Copilot should factor in this pricing shift when comparing total cost of ownership for their team.

tips_of_the_day: Before rolling out Claude Code to your whole team, set a monthly token budget alert using your Anthropic account's usage dashboard — agentic sessions can consume far more tokens than simple chat, and it's easy to overspend without guardrails in place.

Use `claude project purge [path]` after wrapping up a sensitive project to remove all transcripts and file history from that workspace — this is especially important when working with proprietary codebases or client data.

If you use an MCP server for a tool you reach for in every session (like a database inspector or test runner), add `"alwaysLoad": true` to its config entry so it's immediately available without waiting for tool-search — this alone can noticeably speed up your agentic loops.
